columns.column_name: Adverse Events - N (%) | All-Cause Grade 3 or Higher | Treatment
columns.extraction_plan: Refer to Table 3, row "Severe (grade ≥3) adverse events". Extract the N (%) values from the columns "ADT with docetaxel population | SOC plus abiraterone groups (n=347)" and "ADT without docetaxel population | SOC plus abiraterone groups (n=226)". The text in Chunk 5 (page 1) also states: "In the androgen deprivation therapy with docetaxel population, grade 3 or worse adverse events occurred in 217 (63%) of 347 patients who received abiraterone". The definition of safety analysis in Chunk 6 (page 4) does not specify "treatment-related", implying these are all-cause.
columns.column_name_raw: Adverse Events - N (%) | All-Cause Grade 3 or Higher | Treatment

columns.column_name: Adverse Events - N (%) | All-Cause Grade 3 or Higher | Control
columns.extraction_plan: Refer to Table 3, row "Severe (grade ≥3) adverse events". Extract the N (%) values from the columns "ADT with docetaxel population | SOC without abiraterone groups (n=350)" and "ADT without docetaxel population | SOC without abiraterone groups (n=237)". The text in Chunk 5 (page 1) also states: "...and 181 (52%) of 350 who did not [receive abiraterone]..." for the ADT with docetaxel population. The definition of safety analysis in Chunk 6 (page 4) does not specify "treatment-related", implying these are all-cause.
columns.column_name_raw: Adverse Events - N (%) | All-Cause Grade 3 or Higher | Control

columns.column_name: Adverse Events - N (%) | Treatment-related Grade 3 or Higher | Treatment
columns.extraction_plan: I examined Table 3 (Chunk 4, page 11) and the "Findings" and "Methods" sections (Chunks 5 and 6, pages 1 and 4). While "Severe (grade ≥3) adverse events" are reported, they are not explicitly stated as "treatment-related". The safety analysis definition in Chunk 6 (page 4) refers to "highest grade adverse event" without specifying treatment-relatedness.
columns.column_name_raw: Adverse Events - N (%) | Treatment-related Grade 3 or Higher | Treatment

columns.column_name: Adverse Events - N (%) | Treatment-related Grade 3 or Higher | Control
columns.extraction_plan: I examined Table 3 (Chunk 4, page 11) and the "Findings" and "Methods" sections (Chunks 5 and 6, pages 1 and 4). While "Severe (grade ≥3) adverse events" are reported, they are not explicitly stated as "treatment-related". The safety analysis definition in Chunk 6 (page 4) refers to "highest grade adverse event" without specifying treatment-relatedness.
columns.column_name_raw: Adverse Events - N (%) | Treatment-related Grade 3 or Higher | Control

columns.column_name: Adverse Events - N (%) | Treatment-related Grade 5 | Treatment
columns.extraction_plan: I examined Table 3 (Chunk 4, page 11) and the "Findings" section (Chunk 5, page 1). "Fatal (grade 5) adverse events" are reported in Table 3 for the treatment arms (7 [2%] for ADT with docetaxel plus abiraterone, and 8 [4%] for ADT without docetaxel plus abiraterone). However, the text and table do not explicitly state that these fatal events are "treatment-related". The canonical column specifically asks for "Treatment-related Grade 5".
columns.column_name_raw: Adverse Events - N (%) | Treatment-related Grade 5 | Treatment

columns.column_name: Adverse Events - N (%) | Treatment-related Grade 5 | Control
columns.extraction_plan: I examined Table 3 (Chunk 4, page 11) and the "Findings" section (Chunk 5, page 1). "Fatal (grade 5) adverse events" are reported in Table 3 for the control arms (3 [1%] for ADT with docetaxel without abiraterone, and 5 [2%] for ADT without docetaxel and without abiraterone). However, the text and table do not explicitly state that these fatal events are "treatment-related". The canonical column specifically asks for "Treatment-related Grade 5".
columns.column_name_raw: Adverse Events - N (%) | Treatment-related Grade 5 | Control
